prompt: A single, visually stunning image of a massive hourglass made of ancient, cracked, blue-veined ice. In the top bulb, the ice is weeping and melting, with giant droplets falling through the neck. In the bottom bulb, there is no ice, only a deep, rising pool of dark, turbulent seawater. Background: stark Southern Ocean polar sky. No text, no labels, cinematic lighting, realistic water physics, professional photography style, macro lens detail on ice cracks, 8k resolution.
dataset_name: NASA Vital Signs - Ice Sheets (Antarctic Ice Loss)
insight: Antarctica is losing approximately 150 billion metric tons of ice per year, causing sea levels to rise at an accelerating rate—represented as the melting sands of time turning into a rising tide.
